name: Ibis (Portable Python DataFrame Library)

performance.notes: Compiles to backend SQL; performance equals backend (DuckDB, Spark, BigQuery)

agent_readiness.notes: Pandas-like API that compiles to 20+ backends: DuckDB, Spark, BigQuery, Snowflake, Trino, Postgres, MySQL, ClickHouse, Polars, etc. ibis.connect('duckdb://').table('data'). Lazy evaluation — builds query expression tree. .execute() to run. Switch backends without changing code. Deferred execution for query optimization. Selects/filters/joins/aggregations/window functions. 'Write once, run anywhere' for data transformations.

gotchas: Not all operations supported by all backends (check backend compatibility)
Lazy evaluation requires .execute() — easy to forget
Performance depends entirely on backend (not ibis itself)
Less mature than pandas for complex operations
best_when: Data transformation code that needs to run on multiple backends
Switching from DuckDB local dev to BigQuery/Snowflake production
Analytics engineering wanting pandas-like API without backend lock-in
avoid_when: Row-by-row operations (ibis is column-oriented like SQL)
summary: Ibis provides a pandas-like Python API that compiles to 20+ SQL backends (DuckDB, BigQuery, Snowflake, Spark, ClickHouse, etc.). Write once, run anywhere for data transformations. Lazy evaluation builds query tree; .execute() runs it on the backend. ibis.connect('duckdb://').table('data').filter(...).group_by(...).agg(). Enables backend-agnostic analytics engineering.
